title: Detecting BOT Mitigations
concept: Identifying when an agent hits Cloudflare challenges, login blocks, or rate limit pages.

exercises.prompt: What is a common way websites protect against bots?

options.text: By making websites easier to access

exercises.explain: Websites often use CAPTCHAs and challenges to verify that a user is human, preventing bots from accessing their content.

cards.title: What's a BOT Mitigation?
cards.body: BOT mitigations are measures websites take to block or limit automated access to their services.

cards.title: Detecting Cloudflare Challenges
cards.body: When a bot tries to access a site, it might face Cloudflare challenges that ask it to prove it's human.

cards.title: Think of it like a Security Guard
cards.body: Imagine a security guard stopping someone at a building entrance, asking them to show an ID. This is similar to how web challenges work.

cards.title: Example of a Login Block
cards.body: If you try to log into a site too many times in a row without success, you might see a login block telling you to wait before trying again.

cards.title: Myth: Bots Never Get Stopped
cards.body: Many believe bots can always access any part of the web. In reality, effective mitigations can stop them!

cards.title: Understanding Rate Limits
cards.body: Rate limits restrict how many requests a user can make. If exceeded, you see a limit page.

exercises.prompt: What happens if a bot exceeds the request limit?

options.text: The bot is redirected to a limit page
The bot continues without any message
The bot is rewarded with extra access

exercises.explain: When a bot exceeds the request limit, it is redirected to a limit page informing it that it has made too many requests.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Rate limits help prevent bots from overwhelming servers.
exercises.explain: Rate limits are set specifically to prevent bots from making too many requests and overwhelming server resources.

exercises.prompt: When a bot faces a _____ challenge, it needs to prove it's not a bot.
exercises.sentence: When a bot faces a CAPTCHA challenge, it needs to prove it's not a bot.
exercises.explain: CAPTCHA is a specific type of challenge designed to differentiate between humans and bots.

exercises.prompt: Which of these would indicate a bot might be facing a challenge?
exercises.explain: Seeing a Cloudflare challenge page usually means the bot has triggered a protection measure designed for non-human access.

exercises.prompt: What indicates that a bot has triggered a security measure like Cloudflare?

options.text: It is blocked from making further requests
It instantly logs in without verification
It is rewarded for its access attempts

exercises.explain: When a bot is blocked from making further requests, it indicates that a security measure has been triggered, specifically designed to prevent automated access.
